name: Puffy Omelet
recipeInstructions:
1. About 30 minutes before serving, preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. In a large bowl, with the mixer set to high speed, beat egg whites until foamy.
3. Add cream of tartar and continue beating just until stiff peaks form.
4. In a small bowl, with the mixer set to high speed, beat egg yolks with 1/4 cup cold water and salt until very light.
5. Carefully fold the yolk mixture into the beaten whites.
6. Heat a 10-inch ovenproof skillet over medium-low heat in hot butter.
7. Cook the egg mixture for three minutes, or until puffy and golden on the underside when lifted with a spatula.
8. Bake for an additional 10 minutes or until golden and the top springs back when tapped with a finger.
9. Run a spatula around the sides to loosen the omelet.
10. Make a slit part way across the center of the omelet; tip the skillet and fold the omelet in half using a pancake turner.
11. Slide the omelet onto a heated platter.
12. Makes 2 servings.
